channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Serbian foreign minister proposes hosting Ukraine-Russia peace negotiations amid ongoing conflict
item.description: Serbian Foreign Minister Marko Djuric offers to host Ukraine-Russia peace talks, citing Serbia's friendship with all parties involved in the conflict.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ivory Coast votes as Ouattara seeks a fourth term with key rivals sidelined
item.description: ABIDJAN, Ivory Coast (AP) — Voters in Ivory Coast cast ballots Saturday to elect a new leader as longtime President Alassane Ouattara seeks a fourth term, while his key contenders were barred from the race.
item.dc:creator: OPE ADETAYO and CHINEDU ASADU, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Russia kills 4 in overnight strike on Ukraine, days after Trump issues oil sanctions
item.description: Russian strikes on Ukraine overnight left four people dead and several others injured, days after President Trump issued sanctions on Russian oil companies as peace talks in the region have largely been stagnant.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mancini, The Hill

item.title: Trump rips Canada's 'crooked' anti-tariffs ad as possibly AI
item.description: President Trump took another swing at Canada late Friday, calling a controversial anti-tariff ad featuring excerpts from a former President Reagan speech, "crooked" and "dishonest." He also suggested that the video was "possibly" generated by artificial intelligence (AI).
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mancini, The Hill

item.title: Russia faces a shrinking and aging population and tries restrictive laws to combat it
item.description: Russia has grappled with a shrinking and aging population for decades, with President Vladimir Putin viewing it as a threat to national security.
item.dc:creator: Katie Marie Davies, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Tropical Storm Melissa nears hurricane strength and dumps torrential rain on Caribbean
item.description: Tropical Storm Melissa has neared hurricane strength, threatening the northern Caribbean with massive rainfall, flooding and landslides.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Europe fights to avoid being sidelined by Trump’s world-shaping plans
item.description: Donald Trump has seized a central role on the global stage, leaving European allies on the sidelines.
item.dc:creator: Jill Lawless And Aamer Madhani, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Japan's new leader faces diplomatic gauntlet with Trump, China and regional summits
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Just days after taking office, Japan's new leader faces a series of back-to-back foreign policy tests, with a meeting with U.S. President Donald Trump in Tokyo sandwiched between Asia-region summits in Malaysia and South Korea.
item.dc:creator: KEN MORITSUGU and MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Malaysia warns ASEAN neutrality eroding as global powers compete
item.description: Malaysian Foreign Minister Mohamad Hasan has warned Southeast Asian counterparts that neutrality is shrinking due to big power rivalry.
item.dc:creator: Eileen Ng, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Ukraine’s Zelenskyy urges US to broaden Russian oil sanctions and seeks long-range missiles
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy on Friday urged the United States to expand sanctions on Russian oil from two companies to the whole sector, and appealed for long-range missiles to hit back at Russia.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump to meet Chinese president next week for key trade deal: White House
item.description: President Trump is preparing for a high-stakes trip to Asia aimed at testing his reputation as a dealmaker, with a potential trade agreement with China on the line.
item.dc:creator: DIDI TANG and PAUL WISEMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Houthi rebels detain 2 more UN staffers in Yemen as the world body reevaluates its operations
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — Houthi rebels detained two additional United Nations staff members and raided the homes of several others in Yemen over the last 48 hours, the latest in a series of events now forcing the world body to reassess how it operates in the war-torn country.
item.dc:creator: FARNOUSH AMIRI and AHMED AL-HAJ, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Japan's new leader vows to further boost defense spending as regional tensions rise
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi, in her first major policy speech on Friday, pledged to further accelerate Japan’s military buildup and spending and complete an early upgrade of the country’s security strategy as tensions rise with China, North Korea and Russia.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Afghanistan and Pakistan head to Turkey for second round of crisis talks
item.description: ISLAMABAD (AP) — Afghanistan and Pakistan are heading to Turkey for a second round of crisis talks, officials said Friday, after recent fighting between them killed dozens of people and injured hundreds more.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Roadside bombing kills 3 police officers in northwest Pakistan
item.description: PESHAWAR, Pakistan (AP) — A powerful roadside bomb struck a police vehicle Friday in a former stronghold of the Pakistani Taliban in the country’s northwest near the Afghan border, killing a city police chief and two junior officers, officials said.
item.dc:creator: RIAZ KHAN and MUNIR AHMED, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Reporter’s Notebook: A Paris jewel heist straight out of the movies
item.description: A movie-style heist at the Louvre saw criminals scale the museum with a cherry picker truck and steal priceless French crown jewelry before fleeing on scooters.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump cuts off trade talks with Canada over Reagan tariff ad
item.description: President Trump said Thursday he was cutting off trade negotiations with Canada, citing an ad campaign that used former President Reagan’s warnings about the long-term risks of tariffs amid the trade dispute between the U.S. and Canada.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Trump-Kim meeting speculation flares ahead of US president's visit to South Korea
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — The last time U.S. President Donald Trump visited South Korea in 2019, he made a surprise trip to the border with North Korea for an impromptu meeting with North Korean leader Kim Jong Un to revive faltering nuclear talks.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Putin deplores US sanctions as 'unfriendly' while EU joins in heaping restrictions on Russia
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — The European Union on Thursday heaped more economic sanctions on Russia, adding to U.S. President Donald Trump’s new punitive measures the previous day against the Russian oil industry.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: US strikes two more alleged drug-carrying boats, this time in the Pacific Ocean
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The U.S. military on Wednesday launched its ninth strike against an alleged drug-carrying vessel, killing three people in the eastern Pacific Ocean, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said, expanding the Trump administration’s campaign against drug trafficking in South America.
item.dc:creator: KONSTANTIN TOROPIN, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Louvre director grilled on spectacular security failures, including camera pointing away from key balcony
item.description: Louvre Museum director Laurence des Cars acknowledged that security failures, including a lack of perimeter surveillance, allowed thieves to pull off heist.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Putin directs drills of Russian nuclear forces as his summit with Trump is put on hold
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — Russian President Vladimir Putin on Wednesday directed drills of the country’s strategic nuclear forces that featured practice missile launches, an exercise that came as his planned summit on Ukraine with U.S. President Donald Trump was put on hold.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Iraq bans Roblox over child safety concerns
item.description: Iraq has joined several countries — including China, Turkey, Oman and Qatar — to ban U.S. user-generated video game platform Roblox over child safety.
item.dc:creator: Cher Hwang, NewsNation

item.title: Israel 'not a protectorate' of the US, Netanyahu says ahead of meeting with Vance
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Israel's prime minister toughened his stance Wednesday by declaring that his country is in charge of its own security and isn't an American protectorate as he prepared to discuss progress on Gaza’s fragile ceasefire agreement with U.S. Vice President JD Vance.
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Australian prime minister's plane makes emergency landing in St Louis after leaving Washington
item.description: Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese's plane makes emergency landing in St. Louis after crew member injured by falling luggage, suffers concussion.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump doesn’t want ‘wasted meeting’ with Putin as he confirms talks on Ukraine war are off for now
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump said Tuesday his plan for a swift meeting with Russian leader Vladimir Putin was on hold because he didn't want it to be a “waste of time.” It was the latest twist in Trump's stop-and-go effort to resolve the war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE and CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Israel identifies 2 hostages returned from Gaza as residents of same kibbutz
item.description: Israel identified the two hostages returned from Gaza through the Red Cross as an 85-year-old man taken by Hamas and an IDF reserve master sergeant.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Louvre director summoned by lawmakers after jewel theft
item.description: The Louvre is facing questions about lax security measures after thieves made off with priceless jewels.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Kartalija, NewsNation

item.title: Paris prosecutor says stolen Louvre jewels worth an estimated $102 million
item.description: PARIS (AP) — The Paris prosecutor said Tuesday that crown jewels stolen in a dramatic weekend Louvre heist were worth an estimated 88 million euros ($102 million), but that the monetary estimate doesn’t include their historical value to France.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Dad sends 11-year-old to bed thinking he was drunk; boy later dies of snake bite
item.description: The cause of death for an 11-year-old boy in Australia was confirmed last week by the Coroners Court of Queensland.
item.dc:creator: Laura Morrison, NewsNation

item.title: US envoys visit Israel to bolster truce, and Gaza militants hand over a hostage's remains
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Two of U.S. President Donald Trump’s envoys traveled to Israel on Monday to shore up the ceasefire in Gaza, a day after deadly violence gave the fragile deal its first major test.
item.dc:creator: SAM MEDNICK, SAMY MAGDY and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Jane Goodall's cause of death revealed: Report
item.description: Epilepsy was also listed as one of Goodall's conditions, though it's not clear if it contributed to her death.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, NewsNation

item.title: Louvre heist thieves had 'inside help': Former jewel thief
item.description: Lawton described the operation as "very well planned" and explained that before pulling off a heist like this, "you have to know so much.
item.dc:creator: Zach Kaplan, NewsNation

item.title: Hegseth reveals another US strike on alleged drug-trafficking boat
item.description: According to Hegseth, the strike happened in international waters and the three passengers who were on the vessel were killed.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Israel resumes ceasefire in Gaza and says aid deliveries will restart Monday
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Gaza's fragile ceasefire faced its first major test Sunday as Israeli forces launched a wave of deadly strikes, saying Hamas militants had killed two soldiers, and an Israeli security official said the transfer of aid into the territory was halted.
item.dc:creator: JOSEF FEDERMAN and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Israel resumes ceasefire in Gaza and says aid deliveries will restart Monday
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Gaza's fragile ceasefire faced its first major test Sunday as Israeli forces launched a wave of deadly strikes, saying Hamas militants had killed two soldiers, and an Israeli security official said the transfer of aid into the territory was halted.
item.dc:creator: JOSEF FEDERMAN and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9
